bead.title: bead-lifecycle: add stable rule_fingerprint to intake.blocked entries with dedup
bead.description:
PROBLEM
intake.blocked entries appended by readiness lack a stable identifier, so identical rerun conditions stack duplicate warnings on every pass.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2784-2815 constructs the intake.blocked entry without a rule_fingerprint and performs no dedup against existing entries before appending.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend the intake.blocked entry schema with a rule_fingerprint field computed as a stable hash over (rule_id, bead_id, sorted evidence keys that drove the rule). Before appending a new entry, scan existing intake.blocked entries on the bead and skip the append when a matching rule_fingerprint already exists. Keep hash inputs deterministic (sorted keys, canonical encoding) so reruns with identical inputs produce identical fingerprints.

NON-SCOPE
- operator_override field (sibling child bead).
- `ddx bead show` rendering (sibling child bead).
- Adding new readiness rules or changing rule evaluation logic.
- Changing the readiness terminal flow itself.
- Updating FEAT-010/ADR-023 policy text.
- Fizeau concrete route selection, model ranking, provider fallback, or profile catalog behavior.
- Rewriting execute-bead branch merge policy.
- Changing post-attempt review terminal outcomes that already carry concrete evidence for operator action.

PARENT/DEPS
Parent: ddx-cc0641a7.
bead.acceptance:
1. TestIntakeBlockedCarriesRuleFingerprint asserts every intake.blocked entry emitted by readiness has a non-empty rule_fingerprint that is stable across reruns with identical inputs.
2. TestIntakeBlockedFingerprintDedupes asserts running readiness twice with identical inputs appends the entry only once (second pass is a no-op for an existing fingerprint).
3. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/...` green.
4. `lefthook run pre-commit` passing.
